content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P.O. Box 807 • 428 J Street • Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886


July 10, 2002

Teresa Vig Rein
Rein & Rein
715 14th Street
Modesto, CA 95354

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-02-124

Dear Ms. Rein:

This letter is in response to your request for advice on behalf of Stanislaus County regarding the disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹

QUESTION

Are members of the Business and Workforce Alliance of Stanislaus County public officials who must file Statements of Economic Interests?

CONCLUSION

Members of the Business and Workforce Alliance of Stanislaus County, which will serve as a workforce investment board pursuant to the Workforce Investment Act of 1998, are public officials who must file Statements of Economic Interests and are subject to the Act's conflict-of-interest rules.

FACTS

You are under a legal services contract with the County of Stanislaus to ensure compliance with all laws relating to the Business and Workforce Alliance of Stanislaus County, Inc. ("Alliance"), a nonprofit 501(c)(6) corporation. You have been asked by the Stanislaus County Counsel² to obtain advice on whether proposed board members of the

___________________________
¹ Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
² The County Counsel has responsibility for providing legal services in civil matters to the Board of Supervisors. The Board of Supervisors is the code reviewing body for the Alliance. (Section 82011; See Discussion.)

File No. A-02-124
Page No. 2

Alliance will be required to file Statements of Economic Interests. The Alliance has not
been formed. If formed, the Alliance will have a board of between 41 and 47 members
which will perform two key functions: 1) compliance with the Workforce Investment Act
of 1998 (WIA), with funding from the federal WIA program through the WIA fiscal
agent, Stanislaus County; and 2) economic development activities, including business
attraction, retention and marketing, with funding from local government and private
businesses.

You have stated that one of the main functions of the Alliance will be to serve as
a workforce investment board ("WIB") pursuant to the WIA. For example, the Alliance
will designate or procure "one stop operators" for the workforce area and will negotiate
and execute "Memoranda of Understanding" with the required one-stop partners,
collectively or severally, to provide services in the workforce delivery system.
Additionally, the Alliance will "charter" the designated One-Stop Workforce
Development System sites. Performance standards will be established, including a
customer satisfaction index, which will be used to evaluate the effectiveness of the site in
achieving the goals and outcomes established in the WIA's required Five Year Plan.

ANALYSIS

Section 87300 provides that:

"Every agency shall adopt and promulgate a Conflict of
Interest Code pursuant to the provisions of this article. A
Conflict of Interest Code shall have the force of law and
any violation of a Conflict of Interest Code by a designated
employee shall be deemed a violation of this chapter."

"Agency" means any state agency or local government agency. (Section 82003.)
We have previously advised that a WIB formed pursuant to the Workforce Investment
Act is a local government agency under the Act. (Holland Advice Letter, No. I-01-140;
Martin Advice Letter, No. A-99-289; see also Dostart Advice Letter, No. A-00-022.)
Because the Alliance serves as a WIB, it is a local government agency for purposes of the
Act. As public officials,³ all of the members of the Alliance's board are subject to the
Act's conflict-of-interest rules since they will make or participate in the making of
governmental decisions. (Section 87100; Dostart, supra; Baird Advice Letter, No. A-94-
299.) Additionally, since the members will make or participate in the making of
governmental decisions, they will be "designated employees"⁴ subject to the Act's
disclosure rules and required to submit Statements of Economic Interests.

³ "Public official" is defined as every member, officer, employee or consultant of a state or local
government agency, with certain exceptions not applicable here. (Section 82048.)
⁴ A "designated employee" includes a member of an agency whose position with the agency is designated
in a Conflict of Interest Code because the position entails the making or participation in the making of
decisions which may foreseeably have a material effect on any financial interest. (Section 82019(c).)

File No. A-02-124
Page No. 3

Please be aware that whether a separate code is established or the officials are included in Stanislaus County's code is a determination to be made by the Board of Supervisors, the code reviewing body. (Sections 87301 and 82011.) If you have any other questions regarding this matter or need technical assistance regarding the establishment or amendment of a code, please contact the Division of Technical Assistance at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: [signature]
Natalie Bocanegra
Staff Counsel, Legal Division

sections.conclusion:
Members of the Business and Workforce Alliance of Stanislaus County, which
will serve as a workforce investment board pursuant to the Workforce Investment Act of
1998, are public officials who must file Statements of Economic Interests and are subject
to the Act's conflict-of-interest rules.

Section 87300 provides that
"Every agency shall adopt and promulgate a Conflict of
Interest Code pursuant to the provisions of this article. A
Conflict of Interest Code shall have the force of law and
any violation of a Conflict of Interest Code by a designated
employee shall be deemed a violation of this chapter."

We have previously advised lhat aWIB formed pursuant to the Workforce Investment
Act is a local government ageflcy under the Act. (Holland Advice Letter, No. I-01-140;
lulartin Advice Letter, No. 4-99-289; see also Dostarr Advice Letter, No. A-00-022.)
Because the Alliance serves as a WIB, it is a local government agency for purposes of the
Act. As public officials,3 all of the members of the Alliance's board are subject to the
Act's conflict-of-interest rules since they will make or participate in the making of
governmental decisions. (Section 87100; Dostart, supra; Baird Advice Letter, No. A-94-
299.) Additionally, since the members will make or participate in the making of
governmental decisions, they will be "designated employees"a subject to the Act's

' "Public official" is defined as every member, officer, employee or consultant of a state or local

4 A "designated employee" includes a member of an agency whose position with the agency is designated

Please be aware that whether a separate code is established or the officials are

embedding.qa_text:
CONCLUSION: Members of the Business and Workforce Alliance of Stanislaus County, which
will serve as a workforce investment board pursuant to the Workforce Investment Act of
1998, are public officials who must file Statements of Economic Interests and are subject
to the Act's conflict-of-interest rules.
